stem: A manufacturing quality inspection uses 3 parallel inspectors for redundancy. The defect caught requires at least one inspector to function. inspector 1 has a reliability between 70% and 84% (uncertain due to limited operational data). inspector 2 has a reliability of 89%. inspector 3 has a reliability of 85%. What is the overall probability that the defect caught?

ground_truth.answer: P(defect caught) ranges from 0.995 to 0.9974. Point estimate: 0.9962.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 inspectors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.3 × 0.11 × 0.15 = 0.005. P(works) = 0.995.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.16 × 0.11 × 0.15 = 0.0026. P(works) = 0.9974.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
